---
title: "QC metrics and grading"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{QC metrics and grading}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

Metric definitions live in `inst/extdata/qc_metric_references.yml`. Each graded
record contains a stable metric ID, entity and stage, full-precision value,
units, definition reference, expected direction, availability/missing reason,
grade, and grade provenance.

The grading hierarchy is conservative:

1. Hard structural validity failures or reviews.
2. Literature rules only when their assumptions and event reliability hold.
3. Context-matched reviewed empirical profiles using robust summaries.
4. Within-run outliers, which can reach Review but never Poor by themselves.
5. Otherwise Not graded.

Allowed bases are `hard_validation`, `literature_rule`,
`empirical_reference`, `relative_within_run`, `user_reference`, and
`not_graded`. WLS/RWLS weighted residuals are never compared with raw residuals
under a mismatched profile. Matrix conditioning and spectral similarity are
panel-complexity evidence, not automatic SCC defects. The historical ungated
lower-tail spread is labeled **Negative-tail MAD proxy**; explicitly gated
negatives are preferred.

Profiles are `spectreasy_qc_reference_profile` objects discovered from an empty
built-in location, `.spectreasy/qc_reference_profiles/`, or an explicit path.
Use `scripts/build_qc_reference_profile.R` to inventory machine-readable runs,
apply a reviewed manifest, and create a de-identified candidate profile and
inclusion/exclusion report.
